item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think the prose is rather clumsy in a couple areas. Bit too tired to do my due diligence and point them out, but I definitely think you could have written this a lot tighter. That said, uh… damn. This was really fun! Kind of a "too ambitious for me not to like it" situation, I at first thought that the link to the next offset was a link to another article, and tried not to click it so I could "solve" the article, which was pretty funny. Definitely was going to downvote that version of the article, but then the comments set me right and I experienced it in full.

Nobody is an ape with a fedora that passively gains knowledge and can write out that knowledge to be radioactive, who was taken from Wilson's Wildlife Solutions and tortured by (and subsequently escaped from) Marshall Carter & Dark. Like…

How can I not say yes? +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's decently executed, but the four separate pages of multiple formats didn't really make me care about this story. That said, the image of cool, mysterious Nobody as a chimpanzee was pretty great. Still, gotta -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Staff notice: this user has been permanently banned for plagiarism.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, nice catch. I'll fix that, thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh hey, just one thing I noticed today… The dates on MC&D memos are all 15/04/2014 regardless of the timeline, so I think you should fix that?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you so much for localizing the images! I'll admit that I forgot to do so myself…😓

Also, I'm glad you enjoyed the article!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really, really love this challenging interpretation. Good job!!

  • typingchimp.jpg
  • chimpboyo.jpg
  • chimpanzeehell.jpg

Source: Wikimedia Commons
License: Public Domain

Title: Chimpanzee seated at typewriter.jpg
Author: New York Zoological Society
Release year: 2012

Source: Pixabay
License: CC0 Public Domain (See Note)

Title: N/A
Author: DaFranzos
Release year: 2016

Note: While Pixabay has adapted Pixabay License since January of 2019, they also has clarified that any images uploaded before the new license went into effect remains under CC0. Please see here for details.

Source: PublicDomainPictures.net
License: CC0 Public Domain

Title: Hotel Hallway
Author: MALIZ ONG
Release year: N/A
this is truly… an epic monke moment

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, thanks for the feedback! Honestly, the connection to Nobody came to me as a bit of a last-minute conceptual bit before I started work on the draft, and I just included it as another wacky way to interpret Nobody. I'm glad you enjoyed the rest of it, though!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoting, but I personally feel as if this piece could do without the last iteration. The connection to Nobody doesn't feel necessary in telling a compelling story, which the first three iterations do tremendously.

Good job on the writing, though! Definitely made me connect to the character.

item.title: Author Post - Reserved
item.content:encoded:
"There are very deep and restrictive principles that determine the nature of human language and that are rooted in the specific character of the human mind."

~Noam Chomsky

"Give orange me give eat orange me eat orange give me eat orange give me you."

~Nim Chimpsky

Believe it or not, this was initially inspired by a random word generator's prompt: "Page radiation chimpanzee bald lease." It's probably the most fun I've had writing an SCP, and I hope you all enjoy it.

I'd like to give a shout-out to the lovely people who looked through this — stormbreath, Pedagon, and Nagiros
